title: gitleaks gate
outcome: gitleaks wired as a local pre-commit hook (gitleaks git --staged, uses installed binary, no network) + .gitleaks.toml baseline (useDefault + low-noise allowlist for generated registry/session output). bin/ontarch-gitleaks report-only wrapper (detect | staged | dir <path>) dispatched via ontarch secrets-scan + moon ontarch:secrets-scan task. gitleaks 8.30 uses git/dir subcommands (not protect/detect) — adjusted accordingly.
validation: Planted a fake GitHub PAT in a throwaway temp dir; ontarch secrets-scan dir <tmp> reported RuleID github-pat with File + Line (redacted) and exited 1. shellcheck clean.
decisions: local gitleaks hook over the gitleaks-managed repo hook — uses installed binary, no clone/network
periodic whole-repo scan is a workflow-automation concern; the routine here is report-only
token_note: enabling — prevents the secret-in-context failure before it reaches a commit or an agent
